folio.label: 20r
folio.l: To drugge and drawe , what men wold deuyse
And shortly of this matere , for to seyn
He fel in office , with a Chaumberleyn
The whicú æat dwellyngö was , with Emelye
For he was wys , and coude sone espie
Of euäy säuau~t , whicú æat serueth hire
Wel coude he hewe , wode , and water bere
For he was ©ongö & mygúty , for æe nones
And ther to he was longö , and bygge of bones
To don æat ony wygút , can hi~ deuyse
A ©ere or two , he was in this seruyse

note.notetxt: seruyse568s is has a double stem.ODR

folio.l: Page of the chaumbre , of Emelye the brygút
And Philostrate he seide , that he higút

note.notetxt: seide570s is has a double stem.ODR

folio.l: But half so wel byloued a man , as he
Ne was æere neuäe in courte , of his degre
He was so gentil , of condiciou~
That æorugú ou©t al the court , was his renou~
They seiden æat it were , a charite
That Theseus wold , enhaunce his degre
And putten hi~ , in worshipful seruyse

note.notetxt: seruyse577s is has a double stem.ODR

folio.l: There as he mygút , his vätue exercise
And thus w Inne a while , his name is spronge

note.notetxt: spronge579s is has a double stem.ODR

folio.l: Bothe of his dedes , and his goode tonge
That Theseus hath taken hi~ , so nere
That of his chaumbre , he mad hi~ a squyere

note.notetxt: squyere557s is has a double stem, the q is traced in the same way.ODR

folio.l: And ©af hi~ gold , to meyntene his degree
And eke men brou©t hi~ , ou©t of his contre
From ©ere to ©ere , ful preuyly his rent
But honestly & slily , he it spent´
That no man wondrede , how æ he it hadde
And thre ©ere in this wyse , his lyf he ladde
And bar hi~ so in pees , and eke in werre
There was noman , æ Theseus hath derre
¢ And in this blisse , lete I now Arcite
And speke I wyl of Palamon , a lyte
In derknesse and orrible , and strongö prisou~
This seuene ©er , hath seten Palamou~
For pyned , what for woo , and for distresse
Who feleth double soor , and heuynesse

note.notetxt: soor596s is has a double stem.ODR

folio.l: But Palamou~ , æat loue distreyneth so
That wod ou©t of his wytte , he goth for woo
And eke ther to , he is a prisonere
Perpetuelly , nougút oonly for a ©ere
